images.alt: a towering, cascading fountain of liquid obsidian forged from enchanted carbon, its jagged, fractal-like spires arcing violently upward as if frozen mid-eruption—each razor-edged ripple eternally suspended in time. The structure pulses with veins of cerulean and crimson energy, their molten glow illuminating intricate carvings of forgotten runes that twist along the fountain’s base, where ghostly water (or is it mercury?) spills endlessly into a basin of black glass. Swirling motes of arcane light—gold, violet, and electric blue—drift like fireflies through the cavernous dungeon air, casting eerie reflections on the damp, moss-choked walls. The camera angle tilts dramatically upward from ground level, emphasizing the fountain’s monstrous scale as it dwarfs the crumbling stone pillars surrounding it, their eroded faces whispering of millennia lost. Every hyper-detailed inch hums with high-fantasy grandeur: the play of light on the fountain’s unnatural surface, the way the liquid defies gravity in mid-fall, and the sense that this isn’t just art—it’s a relic of a god’s wrath, waiting to wake.

The background is a swirling mix of deep reds, hot pinks, and magentas, with massive brass gears painted bright pink looming over the stage like a twisted chandelier. Thick plumes of white steam intermingle with red glitter in the air. I am posed dramatically on a trapeze bar, wearing a corseted can-can dress that is a chaotic explosion of ruffled tulle in shades of crimson and fuchsia, supported by a structure of visible copper wiring. My black and white hair is teased high into a Victorian updo, interwoven with small glowing red lightbulbs. My harlequin makeup is emphasized with heavy pink blush and dark red lipstick. I hold a fan made of polished metal feathers that look sharp enough to cut. The lighting is dramatic and moody, casting long shadows and reflecting off the metallic textures. The vibe is theatrical, seductive, and slightly dangerous—like a mechanical fever dream in a brothel on Mars.

images.alt: The visual is a surreal, disorienting scene set inside a sterile, all-white spacecraft corridor reminiscent of 2001, but lit with the fuzzy, hazy glow of a David Lynch dreamscape. The white walls seem to stretch on forever, but the corners are obscured by heavy, unnatural shadows that almost look like they're breathing. I am floating horizontally in mid-air, weightless, wearing a retro-futuristic white space suit with oversized, bulbous joints. My helmet is visor-less, revealing my face which is pale and expressionless, looking vacantly into the middle distance. My black and white hair is floating upwards as if underwater. In the background, a distorted, flickering monitor on the wall displays static that slowly morphs into a grinning face. The lighting is soft and diffuse, casting no sharp shadows, yet the darkness at the edges of the frame feels heavy and oppressive. The vibe is eerie, isolating, and psychosexual—like a beautiful nightmare where you're lost in space and something is watching you.

images.alt: a lone girl in a wind-touched white dress standing at the blurred edge of an endless field, her bare feet half-buried in the grass, surrounded by deflated balloons like discarded chrysalides. Dozens of translucent orbs rise around her, each one a pulsing ember—soft, throbbing lights that mimic the rhythm of a heartbeat, casting faint amber glows against her skin. The sky is split violently between dying gold and devouring blue, the horizon a razor’s edge where day bleeds into night. Her expression is neither grief nor triumph but something vast and unnamable - the quiet of a threshold crossed. The shallow depth of field melts the field into haze, forcing focus onto her face, the balloons still climbing, the ones already fallen. The contrast is brutal: warmth clashing with cold, light with dark, ascent with decay—all rendered in hyper-realistic detail, cinematic and aching, as if the whole scene might dissolve into the next gust of wind. The background is a masterpiece of long shadows and swirling silver filigree, soft dark pastel green florals, and celestial accents, forming an ornate circular composition that frames her figure like an ethereal goddess. The soft, painterly shading and flowing, decorative lines merge the timeless beauty of Art Nouveau with the chic refinement of modern fashion, creating a stunning fusion of past and present. No text or additional elements are present. All details are precise, harmonious, and fully aligned with the ArsMJStyle

Going into my 30s pursuing a dream that I gave up almost a decade ago after some truly terrible advice from a college advisor.

I'm a little nervous, but I think that's just the excitement of finally blowing the dust of. It's never too late to learn something new as they say. 💚

I will at least clarify it's not toward any particular person. It's likely by coincidence that so much has been fucking over artists/people as a whole, whether it's life itself stunting creativity or other health issues. I just wish I could do more as a whole, even if it was to help them out more.

I hope I'm never a bother asking for updates on art. I may follow most of those I do get art from, but I don't always see all the updates that come on social media. 

But I'd still much rather know now a piece will be started in September than trying to guess if it'll even be sketched anytime soon.

Donald Trump has said the growing financial pressure inflicted on Americans by the war on Iran is “not even a little bit” motivating him to make a peace deal with Tehran.
